rawPrompt: Create a baseline screenshot library for the surface library source. The plan must turn the request into acceptance-ready work for the built-in surface library. The captured evidence should reflect dark mode during verification. It should publish a reviewable evidence package for GitHub and documentation stakeholders. It needs to leave a visible business process gate for baseline review. Do this so that product and engineering leads can inspect the baseline without reading implementation details.

codeSurface.label: Surface Library
codeSurface.rationale: The prompt points to surface-library behavior inside intent-poc-app.

alternatives.label: Intent Studio
alternatives.reason: The library request may also touch the Studio shell if the prompt is using Studio language loosely.

businessIntent.statement: Create a baseline screenshot library for the surface library source. The plan must turn the request into acceptance-ready work for the built-in surface library. The captured evidence should reflect dark mode during verification. It should publish a reviewable evidence package for GitHub and documentation stakeholders. It needs to leave a visible business process gate for baseline review. Do this so that product and engineering leads can inspect the baseline without reading implementation details.
businessIntent.desiredOutcome: product and engineering leads can inspect the baseline without reading implementation details

acceptanceCriteria.description: turn the request into acceptance-ready work for the built-in surface library
publish a reviewable evidence package for GitHub and documentation stakeholders
leave a visible business process gate for baseline review
product and engineering leads can inspect the baseline without reading implementation details
Intent is translated into executable work for intent-poc-app.
Evidence is captured and packaged for review.
Results are packaged so they can be distributed consistently, with the desired outcome of: product and engineering leads can inspect the baseline without reading implementation details.

scenarios.title: Intent is translated into acceptance-ready work
scenarios.goal: Turn the raw business statement into reviewable BDD structure.
given: A business intent has been captured: Create a baseline screenshot library for the surface library source. The plan must turn the request into acceptance-ready work for the built-in surface library. The captured evidence should reflect dark mode during verification. It should publish a reviewable evidence package for GitHub and documentation stakeholders. It needs to leave a visible business process gate for baseline review. Do this so that product and engineering leads can inspect the baseline without reading implementation details.
The desired outcome is explicit: product and engineering leads can inspect the baseline without reading implementation details
when: The planner decomposes the intent into acceptance criteria and scenarios.
Applicable sources and destinations are identified from the prompt and configuration.
then: turn the request into acceptance-ready work for the built-in surface library

scenarios.title: Behavior is verified visually for applicable sources
scenarios.goal: Define Playwright screenshot verification that QA can execute to validate behavior for each source involved in the intent.
given: Source intent-poc-app is available for execution.
when: TDD planning prepares Playwright screenshot verification for the applicable sources.
The runner maps the requested behavior into QA-runnable visual checkpoints.
then: QA can run a Playwright screenshot flow to verify behavior for intent-poc-app.
Each applicable source has executable visual verification coverage with reviewable screenshots.

scenarios.title: Results are distributed consistently
scenarios.goal: Make the outputs visible in the places that matter to stakeholders.
given: Execution produces evidence, summaries, and progress state.
Distribution destinations are known before execution begins.
when: The run reaches the distribution stage.
Publishing destinations receive the resulting evidence and summaries.
then: Stakeholders can inspect the outcome through a consistent package tied to the intent.
Distribution remains decoupled from any single source-specific workflow.

specs.testName: Behavior is verified visually for applicable sources

requiredUiStates.label: Theme mode
requiredUiStates.description: The demo app supports light and dark theme states that materially change contrast, tokens, and screenshot evidence.

notes: The shared library surfaces and Intent Studio expose a theme toggle that must be activated deliberately.
Stateful UI bugs should declare and verify the requested UI state before screenshot evidence is trusted.
Prefer reusable state activation contracts over prompt-specific routing or one-off checkpoint logic.
Verify the active theme state before trusting screenshot comparisons.
Until a stable route contract exists, do not assume a library URL alone implies dark mode.
requiredUiStates.reason: Prompt requests the Theme mode UI state with value "dark".

destinations.label: Controller artifacts
destinations.reason: Local evidence bundles are always written by the controller.
details: Stores plan, manifests, logs, captures, and summaries on disk.

destinations.label: Linear parent issue
destinations.reason: Linear is the preferred destination for the BDD/TDD plan, but it is currently disabled in config.
details: Carries the business intent, acceptance criteria, scenarios, work items, and execution updates.

destinations.label: Source workspace publication
destinations.reason: Workspace publishing is disabled until artifacts.storageMode is set to 'both'.
details: Useful when evidence should live beside the source repository.

destinations.label: GitHub workflow
destinations.reason: The prompt references GitHub-facing workflow, but there is no GitHub destination publisher yet.
details: Future publisher: PR comments, check runs, or issue updates.

destinations.label: Documentation space
destinations.reason: The prompt references documentation outputs, but there is no docs publisher yet.
details: Future publisher: markdown docs, wiki pages, or knowledge-base updates.

destinations.label: Business process controls
destinations.reason: The prompt references process-level rollout, which should become an explicit destination in the system.
details: Future publisher: approval gates, process checklists, or automation hooks.

tools.label: Linear-first scoping
tools.reason: The runner creates resumable Linear business and source lanes before the detailed planner pass expands BDD and TDD output.
details: Detailed planner output is written back onto the same parent and source-lane issues after scoping completes.

tools.label: BDD planning
tools.reason: The system converts the raw prompt into reviewable acceptance criteria and scenarios after Linear scoping completes.
details: Deterministic by default, with optional Gemini refinement when configured.

tools.label: Playwright TDD generation
tools.reason: Applicable sources now produce Playwright-first executable test plans and checkpoint screenshots.
details: Generated specs are intended for checked-in repo storage with overwrite semantics.

tools.label: Visual verification
tools.reason: Applicable sources currently expose visual checkpoints and capture definitions.
details: Playwright screenshots remain one execution tool rather than the top-level product model.

tools.label: Environment deployment
tools.reason: A generic local or Kubernetes deployment stage is planned but not yet wired into the runner.
details: This will become the shared path for local, minikube, and k3s-backed execution.

tools.label: Implementation loop
tools.reason: Implementation retries are available, but the current config keeps this stage disabled.
details: When QA is also enabled, failed verification can loop back into another implementation attempt.

tools.label: QA verification
tools.reason: QA verification is available, but the current config keeps this stage disabled.
details: The default QA bundle runs typecheck, the deterministic code test suite, and generated Playwright specs when a source enables them.

tools.label: Evidence reporting
tools.reason: Every run produces summaries and machine-readable manifests.
details: Reports become distribution inputs for destinations like Linear and GitHub.

tools.label: Linear publishing
tools.reason: Linear publishing is planned, but config currently disables it.
details: Intended to carry the parent issue for BDD, AC, and TDD structure.

reviewNotes: Source intent-poc-app: Stateful UI bugs should declare and verify the requested UI state before screenshot evidence is trusted.
Source intent-poc-app: Prefer reusable state activation contracts over prompt-specific routing or one-off checkpoint logic.
Source intent-poc-app: Verify the active theme state before trusting screenshot comparisons.
Source intent-poc-app: Until a stable route contract exists, do not assume a library URL alone implies dark mode.
Linear publishing is part of the plan, but it is inactive until config.linear.enabled is turned on.

stages.label: Prompt Interpretation
stages.description: Bound the raw prompt to intent type, source scope, and capture hints before planning.

stages.label: Linear Scoping
stages.description: Shape the work into resumable Linear-owned lanes before execution planning expands.

stages.label: BDD Planning
stages.description: Refine the business intent, acceptance criteria, and scenarios after prompt interpretation.

stages.label: TDD Planning
stages.description: Translate the accepted scenarios into Playwright-first executable test artifacts.

stages.label: Implementation
stages.description: Apply the planned changes against the scoped source workspace.

stages.label: QA Verification
stages.description: Verify implementation output, tests, and evidence before completion or retry.
